desc:
Even at high noon on a cloudless day, it is doubtful whether the sun's warmth
could be felt here. On the ground, piles of leaves undergo the slow process of
decomposing back into the soil from which they grew. The only sound here comes
from the few insects that have made the damp leaves their home. To the east the
unbroken canopy continues.
name: Approaching a Dense Canopy

desc:
The trees continue on immeasurably in all directions. The events of the outside
world seem completely forgotten beneath the awning of branches and sheets of
foliage, that plunge the enclosed land into darkness. The trees are nonchalant
and uncaring and the only sounds are a faint rustling of leaves in the canopy
above, and the skittering of some forest insects taking refuge amongst piles of
debris and leaf litter.
name: Beneath an Enclosed Canopy

desc:
A maze-work of large buttress roots shoot out from the base of the massive
trees. It is nigh on impossible to avoid these roots in the gloomy darkness that
resides beneath the dense canopy. The branches and tree trunks are clothed with
countless years of lichen that thrives in the moist musty air. Silence dominates
the forest. Even a strong gale would bring nothing more then a muffled rustle of
leaves. Away to the north the ground slopes gradually downwards and the forest
grows thick and impenetrable. In all other directions the trees continue,
seemingly without end.
name: Beneath an Enclosed Canopy

desc:
Pervasive darkness dwells like a ruler beneath the enclosed canopy. Large
buttress roots growing through the littered forest floor make travel very slow
and cumbersome. All seems quiet and still, and even the strongest breeze moves
between the leaves in apparent silence. The air holds a musty odour and is cool
and clammy to the skin. The forest continues to the south, west and east. To the
north the ground slopes gently downwards, but travel that direction is suddenly
cut off by a mass of fallen and tangled trees.
name: Beneath an Enclosed Canopy

desc:
It is oppressive and stuffy beneath the dense, overhanging canopy and the air is
cool and moist to the skin. The absence of natural light has left the forest
floor devoid of undergrowth, granting more space for the impeding buttress
roots. Patches of dark tree trunk show through between the covering of
grey-green lichen. Sounds are muffled and quiet. The ground slopes slightly
downwards to the north.
name: Beneath a Heavy Canopy

desc:
The oppressive dimness and fusty, dank air create a dismal atmosphere. Dark
trees reach out their limbs in an umbrella of foliage, completely shutting out
any sight of the sky. Their gnarled bodies are home to a patchwork of frilly
lichens and furry mosses. Numerous insects scurry beneath piles of leaves and
debris which are scattered across the forest floor. The forest continues in
seemingly endless waves of trees as far as the eye can see.
name: Beneath a Heavy Canopy

desc:
Thick musty air and compact foliage make the forest here dense and smothering.
Many branches grow low to the ground, their outermost twigs drawing patterns in
the soft earth. These low branches and the many protruding roots make travel a
journey of careful and selective climbing, ducking and weaving.
name: Forest of Obstacles

desc:
Moss-covered roots spread out from the base of the trees like creeping green
tendrils rummaging through the brown leaf litter. The textured grey bark is
cracked and in many places peeling away, revealing a lighter colour beneath. The
trunks have developed deep wrinkles as the branches have grown larger and
heavier, and the interlaced canopy allows only sufficient light through to
nourish the moss and lichens.
name: Moss-covered Trees

desc:
Dark-grey trunks support a proliferation of branches that reach up like fingers
grasping for the skies. Clinging to these fingers is a plethora of dark-green
leaves that would have appeared glossy in the younger days of the forest, but
are now dull and almost lifeless. The forest floor is scattered with dead leaves
and numerous moss-covered roots creep amongst the litter.
name: Reaching Branches

desc:
Woody broad-flanged buttresses support the base of the thick crooked trunks. In
turn, the trunks support masses of contorted branches that carry large, lifeless
leaves. These leaves were once powerful factories, catching the light in the
sun-dappled lower parts of the forest and converting it into vital energy and
nutrients. Now, the once-reaching branches stretch no more and the leaves do
nothing more than block the light and trap musty air.
name: Lifeless Forest

desc:
Eerie creaks and groans echo between the trees as the interlocking branches move
with the slightest breeze. A number of poplar trees grow interspersed and the
rustling of their fine branches adds to the cacophony. Surrounding the poplar
trees are aged and misshapen trees of many kinds that managed to take root
during the vast history of the wood. The dense soil has been leached of its
nutrition throughout the long years, leaving nothing to promote new growth or to
sufficiently feed the trees.
name: Creaking Wood

desc:
Poplar trees have gathered here in a vast but tight clump, preventing other
trees from developing. Their compact growth and network of arching branches
makes it hard to traverse through the centre of the clump. Each tree guards the
cluster by sending out its strong roots far into the surrounding forest. The
bases of the old trunks are deeply furrowed into firm dark ridges and each
lobular leaf clings to its limb so firmly that it remains attached even through
the dormancy of winter.
name: Patch of Poplars

desc:
Long-established trees grow here, embedded into the littered earth. Their
branches are slender and weak, bowing under the weight of the voluminous foliage
grown over their countless years. The trunks also are relatively thin and many
of the frail forms have crumpled beneath the weight. During winter, the fine
branches on most of the deciduous trees seem to transform into the strands of a
tangled spider's web. Mist condenses on these delicate limbs and glistens in the
light of day.
name: Delicate Trees

desc:
Delicate branches droop low to the ground, no longer able to support their vast
twisted length and cumbersome weight. The trunks also are bent beneath the
burden of their upper frames, and the wrinkled coating of bark has begun to
peel, leaving the rich brown soil littered with the discarded skin. The air
beneath the bowing limbs is musty and pungent with a woody odour. From time to
time, a gentle rustling can be heard through the canopy as the wind blows and an
occasional creak or snap fractures the stillness.
name: Weeping Woodland

desc:
Gnarled trunks and twisting branches are enmeshed in a complexity of tangles.
Winding throughout these tangled masses are thick spiralling vines, their
tendrils reaching out into the cavities searching for the next limb to cling to.
Olive-green lichens tumble down in flowing web-like cascades from many of the
overhead branches. The cluttered foliage makes the forest dim and the air
stuffy.
name: Tangled Wood

desc:
The trees here produce a living picture of health and vibrance. Many young
red-tipped shoots sprout from the ends of the sturdy branches, jostling upwards
to find their place in the sunlit canopy. The straight solid tree trunks are
coated with crinkly brown-grey bark that has developed deep cracks as the trunks
expanded over time. A strong woody aroma leeches out from the foliage and
permeates the air.
name: Vigorous Wood

desc:
A lofty elevated canopy of spreading foliage breaks forth in an expansive jade
and emerald mantle. Beneath this canopy, a sanctuary is formed by enormous
pillared trunks of burnished brown and grey. The forest floor is coated with a
carpet of golden leaves that look so flawless and vivid that not one appears to
have begun decaying. The crystal clear air is fresh and wholesome, and borne
upon the slightest breeze is a light fragrance of hemlock and beech.
name: Stunning Forest

desc:
Untouched by shadow or decay, this virgin forest displays a tranquillity and
beauty rarely experienced in the lands of Middle-earth. Tall trees of many
varieties reflect the purity of the air that they breathe and the waters that
they drink. Each emerald leaf is perfectly formed, and the arrangement of
branches is as intricately woven as a fine tapestry. The forest floor is layered
with a carpet of golden leaves.
name: Pristine Wood

desc:
A polluting shadow slowly creeps from north to south across this region of the
forest. One by one, the trees gradually succumb to this threat. In the north
they droop sadly and defeated, their colour and vibrance lost. However, the
trees to the south have so far withstood the menace. Their robust trunks stand
bold and proud and the branches defiantly display their glorious foliage.
name: A Forest Succumbing To Shadow

desc:
A spreading illness is destroying these once robust and noble trees. No young
growth is visible amongst the ailing forms and the thick, old bark has been
severely tarnished. Many naked branches droop towards the ground, their vibrant
foliage now piled like mounds of drab refuse. Once laden with a fresh woody
aroma, the air is now stuffy and tainted with decay.
name: Feeble Forest

desc:
Storeyed elms and ancient oak trees have begun to be affected by a spreading
malignancy. Their branches sag slightly and the vibrance is gradually fading
from the foliage. The occasional sapling that manages to grow withers and dies
without ever glimpsing the sun. This steady, unending demise shows no favour as
everything is slowly polluted by its spread.
name: Tainted Forest

desc:
The thick robust trunks of various trees show that this is an old, yet healthy
part of the wood. Numerous small plants try to squeeze a place for themselves
amongst the developed trees. Their vigorous growth and bright foliage are in
distinct contrast to the brownish-grey leaf litter on the forest floor.
name: Hale Forest

desc:
Having been expelled from the gathering of birch trees to the north, the oak and
fir trees take refuge here in a scattered assembly. Their stately forms display
a forgotten glory and the branches that they once held up high and dignified
have now begun to droop low. Cracked, woody wrinkles have taken the place of
their smooth, leathery bark and on the ground drifts of leaves prevent new
growth. The air holds such a sweet, almost alpine aroma, that even the stagnancy
here cannot subdue it.
name: Scattering of Oak and Fir

desc:
Dank misty air swirls with fresh air drawn from the pure forests to the south
and west. Fine finger-like branches wave eerily in the eddying air and lichens
growing from the branches sway as though dancing to a mournful lay. Mist, that
condenses on the trees, drips down with little splashes to form numerous murky
puddles that dot the ground. To the east, the forest becomes lighter and the
presence of oak and fir trees increases.
name: Swirling Forest

desc:
Long ago this ancient grove must have had exquisite leaves and strong, powerful
limbs. Now, those leaves have become discoloured and fragile. Once sturdy
branches are now buckled and broken under the pressure of their own weight,
caused by a strong sapping sickness. Slowly the varied colours of the forest
have faded into a pale and dreary grey. Everywhere the touch of decay is
present.
name: An Ailing Grove in the Forest

desc:
The trees growing here are diminishing rapidly, rotting from the inside and then
falling in upon themselves. They are slowly losing a noble battle against the
rampant sickness that has spread through the forest. Dreary stumps poke up out
of swirling mists, like the unmarked graves of forgotten soldiers. Twisted
roots, coupled with rotting stumps and fallen trees, make it hard to find a good
footing upon which to walk.
name: A Feeble Growth of Trees

desc:
The oak, ash, and fir that live in this part of the forest are very near death.
The bark of some of the trees seems to be hanging off in long shreds, whilst
their leaves and needles have lost their colour altogether. A thick mist
blankets the ground here, and makes it difficult to pick a path among the
twisted roots that snake along the forest floor. A sickly smell wafts through
the air, making it hard to breathe.
name: A Sickly, Dying Forest

desc:
The knotted roots of the oak trees here resemble great piles of blackened bones,
strewn around the forest floor. The trees appear to be suffering and poisoned by
some dreadful unknown blight. Limbs hang limp from the occasional sickened fir,
all of which have lost the majority of their needles. In all directions, the
sickness of the forest extends without pity, with no sign of what could be
responsible for such devastation.
name: A Poisoned Forest

desc:
The forest floor seems bare and lifeless, its musty soil broken only
infrequently by lonely twisted questing roots or the occasional dell of decaying
leaf litter. The cloying scent of musty decay is particularly pungent here.
Ancient ash and oak stand tall and still here, their limbs blackened and
leafless, bereft of any signs of continued life.
name: A Pungent Lifeless Dell

desc:
The thick, black muddy soil is sodden and barren. Every footfall accompanied by
sucking squelches that raise a pungent rotten stench. This odour combines with
the forest's already musty environment to make breathing decidedly unpleasant
and difficult. Decayed, rotting tree stumps and twisted black-hearted,
blight-ridden giants surround. For ages unknown they have been hidden from the
sun by a darkness that seems to never lift from this dip in the greater forest.
name: A Black Bog

desc:
As the ground to the south begins to drop, the trees transform into dark eerie
figures. Branches become more tangled, and the canopy closes in. Lichens grow
dominant, hanging like beards from the twisted trees. A stale, smothering odour
of decay drifts up from the darkness to the south, and the dank air moistens
everything, from the clumps of soil to the leaf tips.
name: Dense, Tangled Forest

desc:
The air has become thinner, but breathing is still laborious. The random growth
of branches is knotted and tree trunks lean at strange angles. Finding a path
through this seemingly forsaken wood is awkward and difficult. Natural light is
scarce and the few rays which may pierce the canopy only serve to highlight the
murky gloom.
name: Dense Tangled Forest

desc:
The trees here are immersed in what seems to be an endless mourning. In silence,
they appear to grieve for the fair days of yore, their tears of sap frozen to
their gnarled and peeling trunks. Dead leaves cling to the branches, unwilling
to be forgotten like those that have piled on the forest floor and are now
slowly decaying.
name: Mourning Forest

desc:
As shafts of light stream through breaks in the canopy, the rays form columns of
illumination through the dusty air. The change in ambience is accompanied by a
waft of fresh air which brings a vibrant, woody aroma. The trees here are spread
out and have less lichen and moss to spoil the rich, brown colouring of their
massive trunks.
name: Patch of Brighter Forest

desc:
Twigs stretch out their long, leafy fingers, grappling for warmth and energy
from any sunshine that falls through the canopy. The limbs grow tangled,
reaching towards the sky in spiralling masses. Other branches have broken off
and lay discarded on the ground like antlers torn from a majestic stag. This
scene continues far in all directions until the forest fades into a gloomy grey
twilight, broken only by a sporadic shard of light.
name: Deep into Fangorn

desc:
The trees have been subjected to severe deforestation, in some places entire
sections have been torn down. To the north of the area a large log pile sits.
The pile itself looks to be abandoned judging by the thick layer of moss
covering the wood. Whoever cut the trees has not even bothered to use the timber
they produced. From all around, the sound of a faint wailing can be heard,
almost as if the trees were mourning.
name: At a Log Pile

desc:
Oak and beech trees grow mingled together, lichen clinging to their trunks.
Although silent and still they seem to have an aura of menace about them, almost
as if they do not appreciate travellers passing through. Beneath the piles of
leaves that litter the ground, numerous thick roots lie hidden, ready to trip
the unwary. The thick forest continues, seemingly endlessly, in all directions.
name: Watchful Forest

desc:
The watchfulness of the forest lingers here. The trees stir as their branches
follow any movement below. Lichen clings to the enormous trunks like the beards
of old men. Piles of leaves and debris cover the ground and one must tread
carefully to avoid the obtrusive roots that often block the way. The air is
thick and musty, and carries a stale fragrance of oak and birch. Looking to the
east a line of trees have grown into an entangled web, forbidding travel that
way. In all other directions the forest goes on as far as the eye can see.
name: Watchful Forest

desc:
This hollow seems to have never been touched by sunshine nor starlight, yet the
trees grow thick and strong. Their branches have developed into tangled webs
that hinder or block passage in every direction. With the slightest disturbance,
the sound of rustling leaves ripples through the canopy and an occasional
hissing whisper is discernable amidst the muffled sounds. The ground underfoot
is soft and muddy with small pools of murky water dotting the forest floor. An
acrid stench rises from the puddles and mixes with the stuffy air, making every
breath a chore.
name: Hollow of Whispering Shadows

desc:
Dark menacing trees dominate this oppressive vale. Their gnarled unmoving shapes
are watchful, yet silent. The air is heavy, and filled with the stifling odour
of age and decay. Nothing stirs here and there is no sound from bird or beast.
The ground squelches underfoot as water from a small filthy pond leeches into
the soil and seeps away westward into a foreboding hollow. The forest floor
rises gently to the north and to the south.
name: Eerie Oppressive Dale

desc:
The air is chokingly thick and carries a pungent scent of age and decay. Trees
cluster together according to their type. To the north, a wall of eerie, white
birch trees stands in stark contrast to the gloomy surroundings, and to the west
grows a clump of strange, dark trees which form a tangled mass of branches and
trunks. The soggy leaf-littered ground slopes downwards towards these tangled
trees. Up above, the canopy is completely closed, preventing any light from
penetrating.
name: Under a Smothering Canopy

desc:
The dense occlusive canopy in this part of the forest traps thick stuffy air
that smothers the environment. Breathing is made even more difficult by the
pungent odour of soggy, decaying earth. To the north-east an intimidating array
of tall, birch trees slowly encroaches on the gnarled old trees that grow here.
name: Smothering Forest

desc:
Deciduous trees grow intermingled with numerous aging trees that appear to have
never shed their foliage nor borne more shoots. This mixture of trees allows the
forest to breathe during the winter months. The deciduous trees, which stand
like skeletal remains, create pockets where natural light can pour in around the
bare branches. During summer, the regrown canopy causes the air to stagnate and
harbour a strong, woody fragrance.
name: Pocketed Forest

desc:
The majority of trees that have dwelt here through the ages are deciduous. In
the autumn, they display their conspicuous, scarlet garb like balls of fire
amongst the dull and ageing evergreen trees. Amber and brown leaves, which
crunch loudly underfoot, layer the ground as a reminder of the previous year's
winter slumber.
name: Dominance of Deciduous Trees

desc:
This long-established evergreen wood is dotted with deciduous trees. Their
cyclical change of foliage is the only thing that breaks the monotony of the dim
drab forest. Large lawns of moss and hanging beards of lichen proliferate in the
dappled light beneath the patchwork canopy. Fungi grows rampant and fallen
leaves survive for only a short time before they decay into the dark humus of
the forest floor.
name: Patchwork Canopy

desc:
Ever-changing colours adorn these mighty trees. The bare tree skeletons of
winter begin to flourish with vivid greens as the days lengthen through spring.
Then summer arrives with greater vibrance and the foliage around and above traps
the warm air and fills the canopy with the deepest green. Though the scene is
magnificent the trees save their most glorious display for autumn when they
blaze with gold and crimson leaves and the forest seems to rain with fire. Each
leaf floats down like a flicker of flame and melds into the scarlet sea of
leaves that moves and fades with the seasons.
name: Deciduous Wood

desc:
The trees in this part of the forest grow dense and thick. The trunks in some
places are no more than a few feet apart and their branches grow intertwined,
making it difficult to determine which tree they have grown from without
carefully tracing each limb back to its trunk. The compact growth makes it dim
and stuffy and the lack of any breeze has left dead leaves hanging from the
branches for many years before they fall and carpet the ground.
name: Entwined Forest

desc:
Tangled complexes of gnarled trunks and twisting branches are enmeshed in an
endless struggle for light and position. Winding throughout these tangled
masses, thick vines spiral around, sending out their tendrils in search of the
next limb to cling to. Grey-green lichens tumble down from overhead branches
like cascades of ruffled tulle, and the cluttered foliage makes the forest dim
and the air stuffy.
name: Enmeshed Trees

desc:
Massive trees grow here. Their every fibre holds onto life, defying age with
bold determination. Not a single branch is without a lush array of leaves, nor
has any trunk become hollow or disfigured. These evergreens form an expansive
canopy of rich, emerald-coloured foliage which begins just ten feet from the
gently sloping forest floor. Every once in a while, a dead leaf falls from
above, twisting and weaving towards the bare, moist ground and is quickly
devoured by scouring insects which emerge from the soil when it lands.
name: Evergreen Wood

desc:
Amidst the shadows of decay that pervade Fangorn, there is light here that
continues to endure. An undisturbed forest of phenomenal grandeur opens out in a
panorama of striking colour and form. The majestic trees carry the burdens of
time with dignified boldness, their crowns reach skyward and their branches
stretch outward as though holding the skies. A rich woody fragrance flavours the
air and the warbling of birds is the only natural sound that breaks the
tranquillity.
name: A Grand Forest

desc:
In firm denial of the spreading decay in other parts of the forest, these trees
stand as a vestigial reminder of the magnificence that this forest once had. The
hemlock and beech trees are resplendent in their robes of rich vibrant foliage
and their mighty trunks seem to be a manifestation of the strength of Orome and
the beauty of Yavanna.
name: A Vestige of Noble Forest

desc:
The mighty trees here have resisted the corruption that threatens them from the
north. The leaves are unstained and the glossy surfaces glimmer with the
snippets of light that break through the canopy. Frequent rain or mists often
leave the branches wet and glistening, like a multitude of stars shining beneath
the parasol of foliage.
name: Glimmering Trees

desc:
Sleep-enchanted trees grow here, perhaps dreaming about the once pure and
magnificent days of the forest. The lush foliage adorning their noble bodies
creates a variegated array of textures and shades which crown the sturdy trunks
with splendour. A scattering of leaf litter and large, protruding buttress roots
provide a few scuttling insects with safe cover beneath the dense canopy. The
air is crisp and clear with a faint scent of pine.
name: Noble Forest

desc:
These trees of regal nobility have survived as a mere remnant of this once great
and incomparable wood. Their stunning form nears on perfection. Every leaf and
branch grows with youthful vivacity even though the trees are so aged they
appear to have become one with the earth. The radiance and health of the trees
gives the impression that it is perpetually spring here, however, a scattering
of old autumn leaves on the ground betrays this notion.
name: Noble Forest

desc:
An undisturbed forest of magnificent splendour pans out here like a panacea
against the shadows and gloom that dominate Fangorn. The long ages measured by
the wheeling of the stars have not brought any blemish to these stately trees.
The lush, dark-green foliage of hemlock and vibrant ever-changing colours of
beech show that the glorious days once celebrated in the forest have not yet
been forgotten. The crisp, clear air is accompanied by the gentle bubbling of a
nearby spring.
name: Forest of Unsurpassed Splendour

desc:
Emerald-coloured foliage is swathed about the reaching branches and trunks of
these stoic evergreen trees. In their midst grows a few lush conifers that bring
a haphazard dark-green contrast. The fallen conifer needles form isles of
rusty-brown that seem to float on top of the deep-brown earth. A soft woody
fragrance fills the air and an occasional breeze from the north-west brings a
fresh pine aroma.
name: Evergreen Wood

desc:
A labyrinth of gnarled old trees, entwining vines and protruding roots spreads
out far into the dim musty surrounds. Hedge-like trees with twisting, deformed
branches and dense foliage grow in a confused pattern, diverting any travellers
from a straight path. Amidst this mazework stand drab and drear trees of many
varieties, each one so faded that it is barely distinguishable from the next.
name: Mazework Forest

desc:
The trees in this part of the forest grow dense and thick. The trunks in some
places are no more then a few feet apart and their branches grow intertwined.
Compact growth makes it dim and stuffy, and the lack of any breeze has left dead
leaves hanging from the branches for many years before they finally fall and
carpet the ground. To the south, the forest becomes dominated by deciduous trees
that give the woodland a dynamic, ever-changing hue.
name: Intertwined Branches

desc:
The deciduous trees that dominate the forest form an ever-changing panorama. The
trees stand as bare skeletons throughout winter, and when spring comes they
begin to flourish with vivid greens. Then summer arrives bringing with it an
even greater vibrance. The foliage around and above traps warm air and fills the
canopy with a deep jade colour. Rather suddenly, the trees begin to withdraw
during autumn. The resultant display is glorious. The trees blaze with gold and
crimson leaves and the forest seems to rain with fire. Each leaf floats down
from its branch like a flicker of flame and melds into the scarlet sea of
leaves, which remains until winter strips everything bare again.
name: Forest of Deciduous Trees

desc:
Deciduous trees remain dominant here, but they look thinner and more delicate
than in other parts of the forest. Their weakly frames bend under the weight of
summer foliage and the glorious ablaze landscape expected here in autumn is
nothing more then a browning of the leaves that tumble down into drifts of
discarded foliage on the forest floor.
name: Ailing Deciduous Forest

desc:
The trees here continue rank after rank, fading away into a greyish twilight.
The diversity in the textures and tones are subdued by the thick musty air and
dense looming canopy which consumes any light from above. Layers of peeling
bark, shed leaves and fallen branches litter the ground and crunch noisily when
trampled upon.
name: Twilit Forest

desc:
Trees of many kinds stand rank after rank as far as can be seen. Each of their
shapes and shades seem to reflect an individual nature. A dark stout thick-
limbed founder grows beside his slender daughter adorned in lime-green foliage.
Behind them stands one who wanted to be different from others and so divided his
strength into numerous branches before he had grown three feet tall. Some trees
have taken the appearance of wise grandfathers, leaning on their exposed roots
like an elderly man leans on a cane. Beside them stands one resembling a
delicate dancer with twisting vines draped about her. Each tree is unique, and
the variety is endless.
name: Forest of Endless Variety

desc:
Firs, pines and conifers have taken control of this part of the forest. The
dense needle-like foliage and low branches block the piercing rays of the sun
and trap a heavy pine scent in the air. The rough-barked trunks emerge like
pillars from the sea of discarded needles and cones. Underfoot, the layers of
fallen needles slide against each other creating a slithery surface.
name: Gently Sloping Fir Forest

desc:
Pines, firs and other coniferous trees grow plentifully, but one tree in
particular stands out with its enormous size and majestic stature. Its cracked
grey trunk would take the outstretched arms of ten large men to circumscribe.
The low branches reach far amongst the surrounding forest, its outstretched
fingers tickling trees up to thirty feet away. These lower limbs provide perfect
steps that can be used to climb the tree.
name: A Prodigious Fir Tree

desc:
Dominating the forest here are large and healthy fir trees, which perfume the
air with a strong pine fragrance. The rough, greyish-brown trunks stand almost
naked from their roots up, until about twenty feet above the ground where a lush
green canopy breaks forth. The forest floor is bare of undergrowth, even though
streams of light pour through the canopy when the sun shines. In place of the
expected ferns or shrubbery only needles, cones and fallen branches are found.
name: Gathering of Fir Trees

desc:
The oak and fir trees are strong in this part of the wood, yet an unusual pall
hangs over them. Branches cling close to the trunks as though fearful to extend
far from the security of their stout bodies. A grave stillness dominates the
wood and as the evening rays cast their last light the silence deepens and the
forest becomes mantled in silver-grey.
name: Solemn Forest

desc:
Stagnancy and shadow has grown within this part of the forest. The once
beautiful and noble trees are becoming twisted and tired. Grey-green lichens
trail from their branches in long, web-like beards and the air has lost the
fresh sweet fragrance of a healthy wood. The ground is scarred with channels
where water from heavy rains has coursed downhill to the west.
name: Forest of Failing Nobility

desc:
A dominance of pines and firs perfume the air with a sharp alpine scent. The
thick canopy is proof that the majority of trees are healthy, however, there is
an occasional naked lifeless trunk that bares testimony that not all is well
within the wood. The multitude of pine needles and cones that lie on the ground
must be kicked aside in order to make passage easier.
name: Thick Pinewood

desc:
Silver fir, spruce and pine have overtaken other varieties of trees. They grow
like massive, green wizards hats stretching sixty feet or more into the air.
Many branches grow low to the ground and the clusters of sharp, dark-green
needles spike the skin if care is not taken. Other needles that have fallen from
the trees layer the ground in a slippery brown carpet and occasionally a
pine-cone falls from above to join the myriad that already lay on the forest
floor. A fresh pine fragrance lingers in the air.
name: Predominance of Conifers

desc:
Numerous old willow trees encircle a small glade in a leafy wreath. Within the
glade bubbles a small spring that is crowned with gleaming light. The spring is
clear and blue by day but by night, when all is still, it becomes a mirror for
the stars. On a gentle rise to the north, fir trees take the place of willows
and a fresh scent of pine is wafted in by slow airs. Outside the willowed glade
poplars with raining foliage mingle with frowning yews creating a subdued
splendour in the aged forest.
name: Leafy Glade

desc:
Walking amongst the glossy dark-green leaves, olive lichens and jade-coloured
needles is like stepping through an abstract collage. It seems that the various
trees have been pasted into this dim and dreary picture. Dead leaves are
scattered on top of the rich dark soil, providing homes for the occasional
insect.
name: An Abstract Wood

desc:
A mingled selection of trees grows here. Variegated foliage and irregular trunks
are the common sight, however there is one type that stands out. To the
south-west, the occasional ghostly-white trunk of a birch tree stands stark
against the dim gloomy surrounds. The other trees are leaning, almost as though
cowering, away from their intimidating presence. The air is muggy and is filled
with tense watchfulness, or maybe even fear.
name: Threatened Forest

desc:
Withering leaves fall like raindrops from the ailing trees. The branches are
twisted and have begun to shrivel and die. The vibrant colours of the leaves
have faded and are replaced by a monotonous grey-green, which matches the
lichens that now grow in the place of most of the foliage. Papery leaf litter
rustles underfoot and the shards of light which pierce the canopy serve no
further purpose than to spotlight the dust-laden air.
name: Sickly Forest

desc:
The trees here are closer and more ominous than in neighbouring regions. Other
species have been overrun, and the unearthly white trunks of birch trees have
become the dominant sight. The ground is covered in drifts of papery leaf
litter, and the scent of musty decay is pungent.
name: Before a Threatening Line of Trees

desc:
Silver-grey ash mingle with other varieties of trees as the forest becomes thick
and stuffy. A few white birch trees can be seen stretching out like fingers from
a ghostly wall that runs far to the south-west. Other trees stand in stately
form, fashioned with mottled-green lichen and coated in leaves of various
textures and shades. The forest floor seems bare and lifeless, except for an
occassional insect scurrying away to its home.
name: Thick Mingled Forest

desc:
In this part of the forest, various kinds of trees blend together in a rather
monotonous scene. The canopy only allows sufficient light through in order to
feed the moss and lichen that smothers the tree trunks in a pallid-green
blanket. Many trees here are evergreens. Their glossy, green leaves provide the
only contrast to the otherwise dim surrounds. A few shrivelled leaves are
scattered on the ground and every so often an insect scuttles beneath one of
them.
name: Mingled Forest

desc:
Oak, linden, rowan, ash, fir and various other unrecognizable species grow here
in a disorderly fashion. The diversity in foliage creates a mottled canopy, like
a patchwork blanket stretched across the sky. Similarly, the unique perfume from
each of the trees blends together perfectly to form a rich aroma that stimulates
the senses with its vibrancy, but also stifles them with its intensity. Through
the canopy, shards of light pierce the innate gloom and cast speckled patterns
on the forest floor.
name: Patchwork Forest

desc:
These once burgeoning trees have become a mass of decrepit trunks over time.
Their twisted shapes make passage difficult and cumbersome and their foliage is
so dense that light cannot penetrate. The ground drops to the north into an
ominous area of darkness and a faint, rancid stench wafts in with the slightest
breeze from that direction.
name: Dense Forest

desc:
Nightshade lies deep here. An eerie silence prevails and it seems that the
shadows that reigned before the coming of the sun and moon have never been
vanquished from this ancient hollow. Though this area is devoid of trees, an
enveloping watchfulness still survives. Fungi grow out of the soggy earth and a
rancid stench permeates the air.
name: A Dark Enclosed Hollow

desc:
The thick stuffy air is engulfing as the trees close about in a tangled web of
branches and foliage. Their massive trunks slump under the weight of their
limbs, which are laden with foliage and lichen. The entwining branches are so
dense that a great deal of scrambling over one branch and under another is
required to clamber through the forest.
name: Dense Tangled Forest

desc:
Passage among the forest here is near to impossible. Trees crowd together like a
multitude jostling to escape from the dejected wood. They grow so densely that
light barely penetrates the forest, and only the rarest shaft has the privilege
of bursting through the dim, stuffy air onto the littered forest floor in a
flash of contrasting brilliance.
name: Dense Thicket

desc:
Dull, grey-green shades from the underside of oak leaves mingle with the glossy,
vibrant hues of beech trees. Looking up at this mosaic of colours is like gazing
through a stained-glass window. Sadly, however, this spectacular artistry is
lost in the dim, unvarying scene which pans out at eye level. Lichens smother
the tree trunks with a pallid grey, drawing away the intense silvers and browns
which naturally paint their bodies. The dead leaves strewn across the ground
take on this same grey pallor.
name: Mingled Forest

desc:
Grey shabby trunks of various trees stretch far into the distance. Their once
noble bodies are draped with olive-coloured lichens and their branches stretch
forth into a canopy of intricate lacework. The air is dim and stuffy and filled
with an odour of mild decay. On the forest floor, insects scuttle beneath the
leaves which are strewn about haphazardly.
name: Mingled Forest

desc:
The shabby, silver-grey trunks of ash trees are evenly distributed here. Their
pinnate foliage provides a contrasting rich, green tone. The air is fresh and
the streams of natural light allow ferns to grow thickly on the forest floor. To
the east, the forest becomes much thicker and darker, and a solid wall of eerie
white birch trees appears to encroach from the south.
name: Scanty Forest

desc:
Though many varieties of trees grow here, they all have the same watchful,
menacing appearance. Oak, birch, ash, fir, and chestnut tower above the littered
forest floor. Over the ages, the canopy has grown into an unbroken umbrella of
foliage, blocking any natural light and making the air dense and oppressive. The
forest is motionless, yet the sounds of faint rustlings and whispers can be
heard through the leaves. Away to the south, the ground slopes gently down
toward a small ditch, and to the northwest a white wall appears through the tree
trunks.
name: Dark Forested Enclave

desc:
The endless monotony of the forest is forgotten as the trees give way to the
vivid green ferns that populate this clearing. The sudden clear openness and
crisp, fresh, sweet-scented air is startling. The feathery ferns, which wave
with the slightest breeze, would provide a warm niche to rest in. In the
north-west section, numerous fronds have been trampled on and many stems appear
to have been broken off. On the borders of the clearing, small branches and
twigs stretch out, hustling for any sunlight which streams into the clearing.
name: Fern-filled Clearing

desc:
The canopy has become more open as the trees thin out. The streams of natural
light that tumble through provide energy for the thick undergrowth and ferns to
thrive. The trees are gnarled and weeping with age, and only the bark which
grows beneath the tree's own shade is blemished with pockets of lichen. Fresh
air rolls from the clearing to the south and mingles with the stuffy air from
the forest. A gentle bubbling sound emanates from a small spring trickling up
from the ground.
name: Thinning Forest

desc:
Overcome by the burden of age, many ancient trees have fallen, and their trunks
are now nothing more than rotting ridges across the ground. Their demise brought
a chance for rejuvenation and many generations ago new trees took root. These
younger trees have now aged and their appearance is shabby and drear. Their
branches droop forlornly and a coating of lichen and moss has formed on the
trunks and larger limbs. On the ground, knee-high drifts of leaves have
accumulated between the decaying ridges.
name: Sombre Forest

desc:
In ages past, when the forest was young, this was a dynamic and thriving area.
Time has now past and the trees have declined into old, sad-looking figures. The
lower limbs have broken off, leaving their bare trunks standing like ranks of
sombre statues protecting the gloom. No undergrowth has found root among the
leaf litter, though insects feast on the nutritious compost and decaying
branches.
name: Sombre Forest

desc:
The forest has become drab and almost lifeless. Trees stand in forlorn silence,
as if they are mourning the years when all was brighter and the Entwives brought
joy to the forest. Their branches droop heavily, and many have broken off with
the burden of countless ages. From these scars sap has oozed out and trickled
down the trunks leaving a trail like blood from a wound. The air is dust-laden
and stuffy, and movement amongst the leaves is barely noticeable even when a
strong breeze blows above the canopy.
name: A Deep, Sombre Forest

desc:
Heavy musty air lingers in the overcrowded forest. Low branches hang sadly to
the ground making travelling in a straight line impossible. The old trunks are
lined with peeling bark and in places sap has seeped from the core of the trees
and trickled like tears down their bodies. On the forest floor, a scattering of
leaves creates a mottled carpet that crunches when tread upon. The forest
extends indefinitely into the monotonous grey gloom of countless trees.
name: A Deep Sombre Forest

desc:
Younger trees flourish amongst the remains of some ancient trees that have taken
their eternal rest on the forest floor. What caused the demise of such mighty
trees has been buried with the passing of time. The trees that have taken root
here show by their dreary, worn appearance that they are still old beyond the
years of other forests, and their branches droop forlornly in sad remembrance of
their forefathers which lay here.
name: Sombre Forest

desc:
Long trailing beards of lichen hang profusely from gnarled and leaning trunks.
The massive stature of the trees and their wrinkled bark provides only a glimpse
of their history and a shadow of their age. Dead leaves stick to the old
branches and litter the forest floor. No sign can be seen of any animals living
here, though a few insects scurry away at the slightest sound. Breathing is
difficult in the musty environment and the hanging lichens and dim surroundings
hinder travel.
name: Amid Ancient Trees

desc:
Saggy old trees prop one against another like a troop of old men needing each
other's support. Long, trailing lichens hang from their bodies like scruffy
beards and ragged unfallen leaves cling to their branches like unwanted hair.
The air is stuffy and heavy with a stale, though not oppressive, woody aroma.
The ground is cluttered with dry leaves and numerous broken branches.
name: Amid an Ancient Forest

desc:
Majestic oak trees govern this patch of land. Their massive trunks and
far-reaching branches give only a glimpse of their former glory. The forest
floor, littered with insect-ridden acorns, is pierced irregularly by shafts of
light penetrating the laced canopy. The air is crisp here, and the smell of oak
is reminiscent of the days when mighty forests stretched far across the lands of
Middle-earth. To the east, the forest is lighter and less dense as the
undergrowth becomes prominent.
name: Cluster of Ancient Oak

desc:
The trees here grows closer and more ominous than in neighbouring regions. The
warm, brown atmosphere painted by the oaks is overrun by a dominance of
chilling, white birch trees. On the ground, a layer of papery leaf-litter has
replaced the covering of acorns and the rich scent of oak has deteriorated into
a stench of musty decay.
name: Approaching a Wall of Trees

desc:
Dense overpopulated thickets of ancient birch trees form a solid, seemingly
unbroken, wall to the west and north. The trees stand vigilant and watchful for
any intruder. Their bark gleams an unearthly white against the gloomy
surroundings and their papery leaves grow so close together that any breeze
sends waves of hissing through the dense canopy. About the floor, the forest
roots form a maze of obstacles and away to the south a tangle of hoary, old
trees can be seen. There seems to be no relief from the dense, stuffy air.
name: Threatening Wall of Trees

desc:
An eerie watchfulness is present here and the menacing trees bend their limbs to
follow any movement. The branches hang heavy with thick lichen, dressing the
trees in a shabby grey-green mantle. A distinct absence of undergrowth is
evidence that only the rarest thread of natural light can pierce the dense
canopy. This enveloping cover of foliage traps musty stagnant air, making it
difficult to breathe. The forest appears to run endlessly in every direction
except to the east where a palisade of eerie white trees barricades the way.
name: Watchful Forest

desc:
Oak trees dominate the forest here. Their smooth reaching branches are dotted
with grey-green lichen. On the ground is strewn a multitude of acorns, which
crunch loudly underfoot. Each acorn is infested with insects and grubs gnawing
away at its contents. Speckles of light come streaming in through a lacy parasol
of foliage thirty feet above the forest floor and the air remains stuffy with a
heavy earthy scent of oak.
name: A Stand of Ancient Oak

desc:
Oak trees dominate much of the forest here. Their thick smooth branches are
marred by a covering of lichen. A myriad of acorns crack harshly as the hollow,
insect ridden nuts crumble underfoot. The spreading canopy forms a lacy parasol
thirty feet above the forest floor and speckles of light come streaming through.
The air remains stuffy and thick with dust and the heavy, earthy scent of oak.
The forest continues in every direction, though the dominance of oaks wanes in
the east as the trees become darker.
name: An Oaken Forest

desc:
Ancient trees tower overhead. Long grey lichen-beards trailing from their
massive gnarled bodies give them a regal appearance. Although the soil is moist
and rich, there is a lack of young growth and saplings due to inadequate light
reaching through the dense canopy. The thick, heavy air seems as ancient as the
trees themselves.
name: Amid Ancient Trees

desc:
Towering old trees lean at various, lopsided angles. Their shabby beards of
lichen hang long from their bodies and limbs, giving them a grandfatherly
appearance. Dense foliage shows that the aged forest is still healthy and
growing, despite the lack of young trees that would thrive if only sufficient
light were able to break though the canopy. The stuffy air is almost oppressive
and a faint smell of decay rises from the piles of scattered leaves littered on
the ground.
name: Amid Ancient Trees

desc:
Though in many places the forest takes on a different character, the trees here
are frustratingly monotonous. One more branch to push out of the way, one more
root to step over, one more pile of dead leaves to kick away and one more
hanging lichen tickling the scalp of any who pass. The unchanging forest
provides nothing for nourishment and little of noteworthy interest.
name: Doleful Forest

desc:
The trees growing here are in pitiful condition. Their forlorn bodies droop
discouragingly and their old withered branches languish sadly. Small twigs
stretch out, desperately seeking for the sunlight that seems to have forsaken
them. Nothing palatable grows here. The occasional mushroom displays a warning
against its consumption and the trees bear no fruit. Many large roots jut out
from the base of the trees. They are covered in tacky moss and would be slippery
to step on or lean against. The odour of decomposing leaves fills the air and
the monotony of the daylight gloom is outweighed only by the pitch black night.
name: Dispirited Forest

desc:
There is no undergrowth to hinder passing, not even a fern. This can be partly
explained by the lack of sunlight, it would appear that nothing could penetrate
the thick canopy far above. The smells of the different tree species interweave,
producing a pleasant, relaxing fragrance. Peering through the dimness, more
trees can be seen spreading out in all directions.
name: Mixed Forest

desc:
Old and twisted trees stand here as a sad reminder that the forest has suffered
years of neglect. The nearest oak has sap weeping like tears from its ageing,
scarred trunk. Travel in a straight line is impossible, the trees grow too close
together. Between the ancient boughs, several large pines stand tall as if on
guard, ready to right the wrongs of years past.
name: Neglected Forest

desc:
Small gaps in the canopy send shafts of light piercing through the dimness. The
light has caused thick creepers and vines to take hold here, a tighter and more
fierce hold than anywhere else in the forest. Movement between the trees is very
difficult, the undergrowth must be fought every step of the way.
name: Hanging Vines

desc:
These trees are spaced just enough to allow a few beams of light to enter,
bathing the ground in a pale light and casting long shadows under the boughs.
The local flora has taken advantage of the favourable conditions and the grove
is awash in a great sea of ferns. The grove continues to the north, the other
directions all lead into darker areas.
name: Fern-choked Copse

desc:
The trees grow close to each other, letting little light enter the undergrowth.
Only a few green ferns struggle to grow among the huge trunks, but their future
is as dark as the surroundings. Something seems to move in the shadows, although
looking deeper into the dale where the movement seemingly occurred reveals only
trees, there is nothing there.
name: At the Edge of a Dark Dale

desc:
This grove is a cold but peaceful area of the forest. The trees, mostly pines,
cast deep, dark shadows all around, not allowing much natural light to seep in.
Although the forest seems peaceful and empty, a sense of something present can
be discerned, a sense of life among the trees.
name: Pine Grove

desc:
The gaps between the trees grow smaller, the trees tower high above and the air
is musty and stale. There is a claustrophobic feel to the forest here, it is not
a pleasant place to linger in for too long. There is a loud rustling sound
accompanying every footstep as the blanket of leaves covering the ground is
disturbed. To the west lies a dark and foreboding area of the forest.
name: Stuffy Forest Enclave

desc:
Here the forest is mostly ash and wych elm. The once smooth, thin bark of the
elms peels off in thin, grey-green strips that litter the forest floor. Their
branches do not droop as much as that of the other trees, as they are thinner
and have less weight to bring them down. The key-shaped samaras of the trees
flutter, in slow spirals, towards the infertile ground.
name: A Befouled Forest

desc:
A variety of trees have taken root here, their branches growing strongly toward
the sun. Many new saplings can even be seen attempting to grow in the already
compact forest. The trees here appear to be amongst the tallest in the forest.
This is in stark contrast to the dark patch of forest just to the north.
name: A Hale Forest

desc:
Several birches have clustered together here in a thick group. A few oaks and
several firs have attempted to join the cluster but have been entirely rebuffed.
The trees here appear all healthy, the branches being strong and the leaves
firmly attached. The roots here are all well planted in the fertile soil,
endowing the trees with great stability and strength.
name: A Grove of Birch Trees

desc:
A dense foliage of ferns and woodland grasses cover the forest floor beneath the
canopy of interlocking branches. Distinctive leaves of the oak cover the limbs
of these trees, as well as the great multitude of their fallen brethren lying in
different states of decomposition amongst the forest litter.
name: Beneath an Oak Canopy

desc:
Large colourful toadstools form a striking component of the woodland flora.
Moss-covered twisted trunks strike crab-like poses, with claw-like branches
reaching out to grab at you. The tangled trunks and branches of birch, oak and
ash support colonies of young puffballs and sulphur tuft mushrooms.
name: A Fungal Infection

desc:
An overly congested canopy of dark ash and twisted oak creates a dusky gloom,
unbroken by any intrusion of the sky above. An overbearing stuffiness makes
breathing difficult as the trees crowd around, limiting free movement and
imposing a claustrophobic environment.
name: A Knotted Briar

desc:
Hart's tongue ferns, with their light shiny green colour and broad, strap-like
fronds fill the undergrowth around you. The ground here is moist and the oak and
ash canopy is thick overhead, making for a particularly well shaded area of
woodland. To the north of here, an opening in the trees is just visible.
name: Amidst Tall Ferns

desc:
At a slight earthen rise in the forest landscape, large cushions of pale green
moss and intermingling liverwort form the major components of the forest floor.
The moss is damp and springy underfoot, despite the general dryness of the
slope. The spread of the trees is more open here, allowing more light reach the
ground.
name: A Mossy Rise

desc:
Much of the oak and ash forest in this area is initially difficult to
distinguish. An encrusting lungwort, one of the largest species of lichen,
conceals the trees distinctive coloration and hides their textures beneath a
cloak of bright greens, reddish oranges and brownish growths.
name: An Encroachment of Lungwort

desc:
As the ground slowly rises to the west, the oak and ash woodland gets steadily
thicker until it becomes interspersed with pine in higher grounds. The
mist-shrouded outline of a great mountain range can be seen far to the west
through a slight gap in the trees.
name: A Slight Gap in the Forest

desc:
A great watchful silence hangs over this area as the forest mourns its fallen
kin. Great boughs and the trunks of more than one fallen leviathan mark a great
tragedy in some distant time. The remaining giants standing here are wide and
tall, their bark ghostly grey and their trailing beards of lichen and ivy the
only testament of life.
name: A Ghostly Glade

desc:
Wood sorrels and anemones intersperse with primroses, tangles of brambles and
the occasional hazel, all within a great grassed clearing in the forest. Such a
vast empty space surrounded by the all-encompassing canopy of the forest seems
most strange.
name: A Great Clearing

desc:
Ancient oak and the occasional pale-skinned birch mingle here, their twisted
branches now sporting a covering of dusty lichens in place of the papery leaf
litter that rustles underfoot with every step. A pungent, dusty odour overlays
the senses, irritating the nose and throat with every laboured breath of
stagnant air.
name: Dusty Lichens

desc:
A thick dark gloom of reaching, grasping and knotted branches hangs over the
twisted and deformed landscape. A dank dusty scent fills the air, dulling the
senses and casting a heavy lethargy. The gnarly trunks and boughs of the trees
seem to almost move on their own accord, snarling and grabbing as you pass.
name: A Gnarled Congested Tangle

desc:
The slender supple trunks of irregularly shaped silver birch grow here beneath
an over-canopy of oak woodland. Densely-packed, green-leafed ferns carpet the
forest floor together with an occasional deep drift of papery crisp leaves. Here
the snow-white branches of the birch show a scattering of fresh dark green
leaves, whilst overhead the towering oak branches are mostly bare of new growth.
To the east the land slopes down into a darker region of the forest.
name: Under the Graceful Birch

desc:
Oak, fir, and birch grow thickly here. To the north-east, the health of the
trees drastically declines. Their limbs have begun to droop, their leaves
blackened and shed from their twisted limbs, which have become frail and
brittle. Blackened mouldy roots twist and rise out of the ground, creating a
dangerous path for travellers, whilst a foul stench overwhelms the senses.
name: An Unhealthy Part of the Forest

desc:
Once proud ash is now wasted away, victim of whatever sickness plagues the trees
in this area. Many of the thickest branches in this grove have at some point
become too weak to support their own weight and have snapped. They now hang down
by long strips of ash-grey bark, or lie on the ground like the blackened bones
of giants.
name: A Sickly Patch of Ash Trees

desc:
The rowan trees that still grow here must have been thirty foot tall at the
height of their splendour. Now their limbs droop lazily towards the ground,
sapped of strength by the spreading sickness in this part of the forest. The
leaves of the trees are faded from their once healthy colour to a diseased,
mottled brown. A thickening mist covers the ground, making the roots of the
trees hard to discern.
name: A Wan Grove of Rowan

desc:
The ground slopes steeply down towards the east. The oaks are not as tall or
straight as in the lowlands. They are more gnarled, the boughs dip lower, and
more of the sky can be seen. Little or no wildlife can be seen and little can be
heard except the occasional sighing of the wind in the branches overhead. The
forest seems still and watchful.
name: Forested Ridge

desc:
The ground, thickly forested in oaks and ashes, slopes noticeably toward the
north and east, giving a vague feeling of disorientation, as there is no
opportunity to see the horizon. Looking downhill through the ranks of trunks is
like staring into a deep pool; no bottom can be seen, only shadow. The soil is
firm, dry, and somewhat stony.
name: Forested Upland

desc:
Great oaks surround you on all sides. In whichever direction you look, a
seemingly endless multitude of trunks and boughs stretches into the dim and
shadowed distance. The black, rich earth is covered with a thick layer of oak
leaves from autumns past. Little vegetation grows between the great trunks.
These monarchs of the forest are greedy and the canopy of their branches
consumes too much light for weeds and saplings to thrive.
name: Oak Forest

desc:
Wood sedges populated intermittently by various wild orchids grow through the
general scrub of the woodland blanket. The deep shade provided by the seemingly
ageless oaks, casts a primeval feeling over this quiet glade. Ragged, dry leaves
hang from many of the branches, giving an impression that this part of the
forest pays no heed to the changing seasons of the world outside.
name: A Primeval Glade

desc:
Decrepit ancient trees weakly reach towards the dim light of the covered sky far
overhead, while paper-like brown leaves occasionally show on their brittle twigs
and branches. Here the cycle of life has stalled and passed the forest by, the
seasons forgotten. A long drawn out twilight remains. To the west the trees
stretch alongside the base of a steep rise, preventing easy travel.
name: A Twilight Forest

desc:
The forest cover here is dense but not complete and where light has trickled
through the canopy, a wild undergrowth of tangled nettles and towering ferns
covers the forest floor. As one passes through the undergrowth, no trails exists
to follow, nor are bare patches of relief to be seen.
name: A Tangle of Ferns and Nettles

desc:
Gnarled and twisted branches overlap and intertwine here as great oak and large
leaved lime make passage difficult and strenuous. The air smells musty, feels
thick with age and seems to weigh you down. The closeness of everything in this
forest threatens to strangle the breath of any traveller.
name: Thick Tangled Forest

desc:
A dense canopy overhangs this part of the forest, trapping thick, stuffy air
that smothers every breath. Dried, withered, brown leaves hang from the heavy
aged branches and gnarled, twisted, finger-like twigs of the ancient oak. The
constant rustle of dried leaf litter accompanies your every footfall, breaking
the cloister-like silence of the forest.
name: A Smothering Heavy Canopy

desc:
Woodland beeches stand along the top of a steeply sloping depression leading
down into the east. At the top of this slope the trees are old and worn, with
browned dried-up leaves and wreaths of trailing lichens. Most are still hale and
deeply rooted, although in contrast, the trees in the dark depression below are
mottled and sickly looking greatly at odds with the remainder of the forest.
name: On the Edge of a Depression

desc:
The forest here seems to be suffering from some kind of ailment. A foul scent
rides the air, wafted along by a light mist as the ground slopes down from the
north and west. Sick as the trees here seem to be, to the east they are even
further along in whatever malady is spreading through this part of the forest.
name: A Sickened Patch of Forest

desc:
The limbs of these feeble, sagging trees droop sadly towards the ground, as if
the forest here has lost the will to live. Bark peels back from the trunks and
roots of the trees, exposing sickly looking decaying wood beneath. A low,
obscuring mist covers the ground, making it hard to avoid tripping over knotted
roots and sudden hollows.
name: A Grove of Weakened Trees

desc:
Tall, sick oak trees populate this area of the forest. The trees are dying,
their leaves coloured with sickly mottled colours and blackened edges. Above all
hangs a reek of some indefinable odour. A light mist dips down into the lower
parts of the forest, making it hard to walk without tripping over unseen roots.
name: A Poisoned Grove of Oak Trees

desc:
The dying leaves of the blackened twisted trees emit an extremely sickly odour.
The smell of death wafts through the air, carried by a light mist that cloaks
the forest floor. The mist has a soft, green glow to it, very faint yet eerily
distracting.
name: A Stench of Decay

desc:
The floor of the forest descends steeply towards the south and the east. The
trees here grow straight and strong, even where the slope of the forest floor
falls sharply. Looking over the edge of a small cliff, you see trees that look
as if they have died. The leaves of the trees have fallen and their limbs appear
weak. A malodorous smell reeks in from the south and east.
name: A Small Drop in the Forest

desc:
Oak, rowan, ash and fir together with a plethora of other plant-life grow here.
Natural light is filtered down through overlapping layers of tree canopy
interspersed with bearded lichens and trailing vines. A musty, heavy scent
overwhelms the senses, forcing one to take deep breaths to get past the
stuffiness of the air.
name: Deep within the Forest

desc:
Laid down to rest among auburn-gold sheets of delicate leaves and musty loam, to
bathe in a pool of fungal kisses, arms poised with early-light gestures, a once
majestic giant lies now at rest. Its forest brethren stand apart, a respectful
distance from their fallen comrade, their mournful tears of respect the steady
fall of faded weather-worn leaves.
name: A Mournful Glade

desc:
The heavy scent of ancient wood and musty earth labours breathing in this
thickly overlapping and mixed woodland. Ancient oak intermingled with ash,
elder, hornbeam and birch stand tall and massive, old beyond belief, wreathed in
aged leaves and trailing thick ivy. The trees seem to be almost aware of your
presence, making them appear watchful and wary.
name: The Deep Forest

desc:
An abundance of dead wood lies at your feet, moss shrouded and rotting with the
passing of the seasons. A steadily thickening stand of oak and beech gives
evidence that new woodland has slowly replaced the fallen monoliths that provide
such a fine meal to the beetles and termites that feast here.
name: A Maturing Thicket

desc:
Short grasses carpet the ground, covering the fallen branches of ages past. The
ash, oak and beech woodland is quiet and dull, the sky obscured within its
densely packed folds. A musty scent of great age overwhelms the senses.
name: Enfolding Foliage

desc:
Holly and ivy are to be found in great masses, growing here with wild abandon
amongst the oak woodland so prevalent in this part of the forest. Plant-life
neither flowers in this ageless wood, nor shows its fruit, perhaps due to the
heavily shaded conditions imposed by the dense foliage overhead.
name: Holly and Ivy Abounds

desc:
In a dell in the greater forested landscape, a natural spring has been cleverly
captured so as to cascade down over a series of moss-covered boulders before
playfully splashing over the stones into a small pool formed by huge, carefully
placed rocks. The trees overhead, and to three sides, appear to cradle the pool
and spring, growing around the pool and almost providing a crude shelter.
name: A Calm Sanctuary

desc:
On a slight rise in the forest, a group of aged pine stands out from the
predominant oak and ash. The great age of the trees here is evident in their
girth, thick bark and the long trails of sticky scented sap that have fallen
over many years in a long trail down their immense trunks. To the west the pine
gives way to a densely packed multitude of species, forming a natural barrier to
travel.
name: A Stand of Pine

desc:
Silvery-grey barked trees standing over ninety foot in height stand here,
interspersed with more common ash and sycamore. These strongly rooted trees have
hard yellow-white heartwood and unlike their more numerous kin, the common elm,
that have toothed leaves, distinguishing them as wych elm.
name: Wych Wood

desc:
Great trailing beards of lichen hang from massive ancient trees, blowing and
swaying in the breeze. Dim light trickles down through the wildly dancing
branches overhead. Mosses cover the roots and climb the trunks of the great
trees, whilst everywhere one looks, a multi-coloured leaf patchwork of green and
brown, yellow and orange rustles at your feet.
name: Trailing Lichens

desc:
All colours have faded from the weatherworn leaves here. Few still tenaciously
cling to the tree, although they have mostly shrivelled and dried. Having
reached the final stage of life, many relinquish their hold on the ancient trees
and succumb to the pressures of age. A gentle breeze catches them one by one,
nudging them in spirals as they flutter and float to the ground.
name: A Steady Leaf-fall

desc:
While standing amongst intermingled oak and poplar all in fair health, it is
hard to imagine what can be wrong with the great swathe of forest that lies dark
and twisted below to the south and east of here. A foul, malodorous, sickly
stench rises from the forest, stinging the eyes and gagging the throat.
name: Stand of Healthy Trees

desc:
Deeply wrinkled trunks and branches whirl in knotted tangles where fallen
branches stretch no more. This once hale woodland has succumbed to an unnatural
blight, which hangs heavy over the forest with a dank stench of decay and
despair. The few remaining blackened leaves on the twisted dying branches now do
little more than block out the light and trap the foul musty air that fills this
shadowy, dying dell.
name: An Unnatural Blight

desc:
The tall poplar and massive oak trees that frequent this region of the woodland
are under siege by a dark decaying blight that is slowly invading from the
south. There the land slopes downwards into a dark, sickly smelling hollow. Here
the trees still retain much of their strength and vitality, yet the decaying
touch of blackened mould is occasionally visible on leaf and root.
name: A Besieged Forest

desc:
A grove of tall upright poplar stands at attention in the midst of the greater
forest. The trunks of these trees all appear to be perfectly straight and
healthy, with a dense and vigorous covering of foliage.
name: A Column of Poplar

desc:
Prickly branches layer grey boles, while lichen, like old men's beards, drips
down and encircles every tree. Light from high above scatters down, forming
pools of darkness and speckles of green on the carpet of needles and leaves.
Alone amongst its ancient brethren, being lightning scarred and shattered,
stands a wounded oak, its iron boughs bent and battered. Still it stands defiant
and majestic.
name: A Wounded Oak

desc:
Moss-covered roots support thick, crooked trunks of roughly textured grey bark,
cracked and peeling in numerous places exposing lighter sapwood beneath. The
interlaced canopy overhead allows only limited light through its clutches,
barely enough to provide nourishment for the mosses and lichens that live within
this forest's grasp.
name: Moss-Shrouded Woodland

desc:
The verdant grasses, here beneath the shifting forest canopy, are filled with
tiny flowers, sparkling like many-coloured stars in a firmament of green. Pale
greens and silver boughs shift in the wind. The rustle of the forests
innumerable green, brown, red and gold leaves is like a spell of music that
entrances the senses and brings to mind what this most ancient of forests must
have been like in its youth.
name: An Enchanted Forest

desc:
Huge splintered boulders, moss-shrouded and root-entangled, stand out from the
undergrowth of the vine covered ground. Ferns, nettles, brambles and wiry
grasses surround these great stones, whilst tall trunks and interwoven branches
of beech, oak and ash loom overhead.
name: Looming Forest

desc:
Oak, ash, beech and birch are all to be found in this part of the forest, but no
tree is more prevalent here than the broad-leafed lime. The trees' straight
thick trunks topping forty yards in height, their strong gnarled branches rising
up to a broadly dome-shaped thick crown. To the south the trees become more and
more packed, gnarly branches interweaving to block southward passage.
name: Broad-Leafed Lime

desc:
Ivy trails across much of the forest floor, criss-crossing this woodland.
Together with a soil that seems damp, heavy and continuously clogging underfoot,
travel here is hard going. Much of the oak woodland has become interspersed with
wildly sprouting young and semi-mature hazel.
name: Oaks and Ivy

desc:
The branches growing from the rough-barked trunk are still strong and solid
halfway up this enormous tree. Their coarse bark and convenient spacing provides
ideal climbing and an adequately stable place to rest. The branches continue
upwards, but become thinner and more fragile. Gazing beyond the dense,
needle-like foliage just above this height, the outlines of trees waving to the
gentle currents of the wind can be seen.
name: Halfway up an Enormous Fir Tree

desc:
Reaching the flimsy branches at the apex of this enormous fir tree brings not
only an uninterrupted view of the surrounding lands, but also freedom from the
isolation of the forest. The warmth of the sun and the soft light of the moon
and stars are a stark contrast to the forest's gloom. A fresh breeze floats by
in an unhindered stream as small twittering birds dash in and out of the canopy
below. Extending for many miles beyond this place is the panoramic vista of
Fangorn. Its complex, green patchwork incorporates every shade and tone of green
imaginable. The vast, green plains of Rohan stretch along the farthest horizon
to the south and east and the majestic snow-covered peaks of the Misty Mountains
arise from the forest about fifty miles to the west.
name: Atop the Forest Canopy

desc:
This pathway is heavily littered with drifts of undisturbed dead leaves. The
increasing shadows and the smothering wall of birch has had a disturbing effect
on this avenue of beech. These ancient trees were once aisles of glossy
evergreen boughs welcoming the Shepherds of the Trees into a homely abode at the
southern end of the path. Now the trunks are dark and ominous, and the remains
of their smooth silver bark is cracked and peeling. The ground is uneven and is
covered with the fractured limbs and fallen leaves of the beech trees which line
the avenue. It is apparent that this area has not had any visitors for many long
years.
name: An Avenue of Decrepit Beech

desc:
In an age not remembered by many, an array of silver-barked beech trees were
planted to form this protective circle. Though these noble trees look decrepit
and nigh on ruined, the elaborately arranged branches and thick solid trunks are
evidence that they were once lovingly tended. The ground is level but strewn
with rocks and scattered with dry leaves that crunch underfoot. A pile of
decaying ferns lay on the western side of the enclosure. Along the southern side
a spring noiselessly bubbles water from beneath the ground.
name: A Ruined Dwelling

desc:
This shallow hole has barely enough room for a child to stand in. Dug out of the
rich composted soil long ago, the sides of this hollow have become crumbly and
can easily be scraped away. The earth along the southern edge is more compact
and long marks have been scratched out its surface. It looks like huge fingers
worked tirelessly to gouge out the soil. On the bottom of the hole, the crumbled
earth has formed a thick powdery layer.
name: A Cramped Musty Hole

desc:
A long and narrow, earth-filled tunnel stretches into the milky darkness.
Diseased roots are visible throughout the walls, victims of an unknown source
that is poisoning the flora of the area. Decaying pieces of vegetation are
littered on the ground in this tunnel.
name: A Decaying Root-Filled Passage

desc:
As the tunnel twists and bends, steady feet are required to negotiate the
serpentine curves and the muddy floor. The air is still and damp. An odour of
death and decay lingers in the dark. The tunnel coils south and east.
name: Decaying Tunnel

desc:
At a turn in this dark, dank tunnel, death is palpable. Dead, rotting roots
protrude from every surface. Spider webs are widely strung from one jagged
surface to the next. Small skeletal corpses, picked clean of every bit of flesh,
litter the ground.
name: A Dark, Rounded Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel narrows drastically, hampering your very movements. The smell of
death, once subtle, has grown noticeably stronger. An assortment of bones and
rotted flesh become more conspicuous with each step taken. The tunnel twists
north and west.
name: Decaying Tunnel

desc:
A large, web-filled cavity has been burrowed out of the ground. Cocoons and
skeletal remains of various size are laden within the chamber, the bulk of which
appear to hold the remains of small animals. However, the odd one seems to be
almost human sized. The only discernable exit is back the way you came.
name: A Damp Dark Chamber

desc:
This once massive tree has been subject to an age of decay. Insects and grubs
have hollowed out the trunk sufficiently to allow enough height for a man to
stand up straight. The stench of the dank rotting wood is oppressive and the
flakes of old mouldy wood crumble from the remains when touched. The hollow
extends about six yards along the length of the trunk and is at most three yards
wide.
name: Inside a Fallen Tree

desc:
Eerie white-trunked birch trees stand in a solid wall about the eastern and
northern boundaries. The trees are vigilant, though not hostile. They appear to
pose no threat but watch every movement made in the darkness below them. The
thin air is almost too scant to breathe, and the hissing rustle from above makes
the atmosphere tense.
name: Encroaching Wall of Trees

desc:
Stark white tree trunks stand in marked contrast with the gloomy surrounds.
Their formation in a massive impenetrable barrier to the north and east is a
reminder that evil forces are affecting this once strong and noble forest. Dark
patches, such as this one, are developing and growing. Even so, the thin musty
air is still laden with the stale scent of noble trees in more beautiful times.
Nothing seems to have disturbed the forest floor for quite some time, and
insects scurry away amongst the leaves and roots. Fungi of various colours grow
rampant on the numerous pieces of fallen wood and debris.
name: Encroaching Wall of Trees

desc:
The dreary cheerless trees that grow here are like menacing disfigurements of
their once noble forms. Oak, birch, ash, and other varieties spread their limbs
in an enveloping canopy high above the forest floor. The lack of natural light
reaching through the canopy inhibits the growth of any new plants or
undergrowth. Instead, the forest floor is strewn with ageless piles of fallen
leaves and debris. A lingering fragrance of each tree variety can be discerned
above the musty trapped air. The ground slopes gently away to a small ditch to
the south, and to the north-east a white wall appears through the tree trunks.
name: Dark Forested Enclave

desc:
Dark menacing trees stand still and quiet, like statues watching and guarding
their stronghold. Their massive branches spread across the ceiling of the
forest, shutting out all natural light and weighing the air down with a stuffy
stagnancy. The waterlogged earth squelches underfoot as a stench of rotting
vegetation permeates the air. The ground slopes almost imperceptibly upwards to
the north and south.
name: Eerie Oppressive Dale

desc:
Stark white birch trees stand in marked contrast with the dark and eerie
surrounds. They form a massive barrier to the north, south and east, hemming in
the evil trees and shadows of this encroaching dark patch. This area must have
been recently overcome by the formidable birch trees because the thin, musty air
is still laden with the stale scent of noble trees in more beautiful times.
Nothing seems to have disturbed the forest floor for quite some time. Insects
scurry away amongst the fallen leaves and fungi of various colours grow rampant
on fallen pieces of wood.
name: Encroaching Wall of Trees

desc:
An enormous black oak looms overhead. Its gnarled bark appears charred, and
little of its foliage is left. All around you lie withered branches and twigs as
if the entire area had been the victim of decay and evil. Marks on the ground,
as from a battle, can clearly be seen. The air is both dry and thick, nearly
suffocating. The gloom deepens, as the forest thickens, in all directions but to
the east where more of the sick forest stands.
name: A Great Black Oak Tree

desc:
An enormous, brooding, black oak looms overhead upon a great bole on knotted
roots. Its gnarled bark appears charred, but on closer inspection is extremely
leathery and tough. A huge twisted mass of roots creep and crawl in every
direction from the base of the tree, snarling underfoot. The oak's thick, dark
limbs reach out to grasp at you like the bony hands of death, as it deeply
shadows the glade around its base.
name: A Great Black Oak Tree
